title: Podman becomes randomly unusable for some users
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

After running some containers, podman becomes unusable for some user. In our workflows it's root user

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Currently, I have no idea why the problem happens. Just the only workaround we found is to reinstall the node. Removing `/var/lib/containers/` does not change anythging and root user has no `~/.config`, `~/.local`, `~/.cache`

EDIT: Finally I figured out, the problem appears if an usb block device is connected to the node...

**Describe the results you received:**
`ERRO[0000] [graphdriver] prior storage driver overlay failed: kernel does not support overlay fs: 'overlay' is not supported over extfs at "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay": backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver 
Error: kernel does not support overlay fs: 'overlay' is not supported over extfs at "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay": backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver`


**Describe the results you expected:**
Expecting `podman ps` to work as `root` (stil working as `ubuntu`)

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.3
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical ubuntu 22

comments.body:
> EDIT: Finally I figured out, the problem appears if an usb block device is connected to the node...

does it affect the file system under `/var/lib/containers/storage`?  Or where is it mounted?

To answer the USB is not really directly mounted. When the device is connected a script does mknod, set permissions and the mount inside the container.

I did more test and the problem does not happen all the time. The workaround works when the problem occurs but having the USB drive connected does not cause the problem.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Sorry this got lost because of the stale-issue flag.
@RomainLyon1 is this still an issue>

Hello,

I'm currently not able to access these  machines, they are in a temporally closed area (for weeks). When I will be able to access it again I will test and update you in return.
@RomainLyon1, did you get access to the machines?

I got access back but I didn't manage to reproduce the issue. I did several tests and it always boot successfully. But the podman API is currently is 3.4.4+ds1-1ubuntu1.22.04.2. Do you want me to downgrade and try again?
I think we can close the issue then.  Feel free to reopen in case it happens again.
